Title: Hello darkness, my old friend: 3-KETOACYL-COENZYME A SYNTHASE4 is a branch point in the regulation of triacylglycerol synthesis in Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch point; upstream; rate-limiting step of VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; haplotype analysis; CRISPR-Cas9 mutant analysis; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; heterologous expression in yeast; VLCFA profiling; leaf wax analysis; confocal localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in darkness and heat+darkness; lower in HD than CHD
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Combined heat and darkness (32°C plus 24 h darkness); extended darkness (3 d, 6 d); carbon starvation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Natural accessions including Col-0, Pro-0, Petergof, Per-1, Si-0, Bu-8, Sh-0
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Very-long-chain fatty acid elongation; cuticular wax biosynthesis; triacylglycerol remodeling under carbon starvation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C24 and C26 saturated very-long-chain acyl-CoAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural allelic variation at KCS4 underlies differential accumulation of polyunsaturated TAGs under heat+dark and extended darkness. 2. KCS4 encodes an ER-localized functional 3-ketoacyl-CoA synthase with specificity for C22 and C24 saturated acyl-CoAs and elongates to C24/C26 VLCFAs. 3. The stronger KCS4(Met) allele promotes higher leaf wax accumulation and higher puTAG accumulation, while mutation reduces puTAGs and increases biomass.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyunsaturated TAGs serve as alternative carbon/energy stores under carbon starvation; waxes contribute to cuticular barrier
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: VLCFA elongation branch competing with TAG formation for saturated acyl-CoA pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses malonyl-CoA with long/very-long-chain saturated acyl-CoA substrates during fatty acid elongation, extending C22 to C24 and C24 to C26
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher KCS4 activity is associated with reduced biomass; redirects saturated acyl flux to wax/VLCFA branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Downregulated in darkness and HD in Arabidopsis Col-0 time-course
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; lipid bodies for puTAGs; leaf cuticle for waxes
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KCS condensing enzyme family; very-long-chain fatty acid elongase component
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Lipidomics by LC-MS; GWAS using 250K SNP chip; regional resequencing from 1001 Genomes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts saturated acyl-CoA flux toward VLCFA/wax synthesis and thereby promotes relative accumulation of polyunsaturated TAGs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Polyunsaturated triacylglycerols; very-long-chain fatty acids; leaf cuticular waxes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes VLCFA and wax synthesis and increases accumulation of polyunsaturated TAGs under carbon starvation, with KCS4(Met) stronger than KCS4(Thr)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in Arabidopsis; transient heterologous expression constructs in Nicotiana benthamiana (specific promoter NA)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First and often rate-limiting step of fatty acid elongation; established for KCS family and supported here as key branch control point
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lead causal coding polymorphism is nonsynonymous SNP m11503 causing Met/Thr change; promoter polymorphisms affect HSFA2 binding but were not causal for puTAG variation; region shows negative Tajima's D and signatures of selection

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation of KCS4 in VLCFA elongation/wax and TAG remodeling context
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Polyunsaturated triacylglycerols; very-long-chain fatty acids; cuticular waxes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcription factor binding to KCS4 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds KCS4 promoter heat shock element; T allele at promoter SNP m11502 shows stronger competitor effect/binding inference
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promoter binding potential may modulate KCS4 expression, but does not explain puTAG variation under HD
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not decisive for natural puTAG variation in this study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HSFA2 binds in vitro to a heat shock element in the KCS4 promoter. 2. Promoter SNP m11502 alters binding affinity. 3. Despite this, promoter variation/HSFA2-related binding was not causal for puTAG variation under HD.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because experimentally validated promoter regulator, but it is not the main causal gene for the metabolic phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value based on this paper because binding variation was not causal
